title: roscoea × beesiana
intro: Roscoea × beesiana, also known as the Chinese gladiolus, is a hybrid plant species within the ginger family, native to China and Southeast Asia. The plant's roots and rhizomes have been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries, possessing medicinal properties that include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Roscoea × beesiana include its ability to treat colds, coughs, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant properties, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and rhizomes are often dried and made into teas, infusions, or added to soups and other medicinal concoctions.
